data.title: CZ posts video of himself playing with Broccoli
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News: Binance co-founder CZ posted a video of himself playing with Broccoli on the X platform, with the caption: "Don't underestimate a Chinese who has a pet dog named Broccoli."

data.title: PlanB Transfers Bitcoin To ETF For Convenience
data.description: According to BlockBeats, on February 15, PlanB announced that he has transferred his Bitcoin holdings into an ETF. He acknowledged the common saying, "Not your keys, not your coins," but expressed that managing Bitcoin in the same manner as stocks and bonds is more convenient for him. Additionally, he mentioned that not having to worry about private keys provides him with peace of mind, suggesting a shift away from being a staunch Bitcoin believer.

data.title: Planet Evening News
Binance Co-Founder Yi He Opens Trading Profile Revealing Investment Strategy and Long-Term Airdrop Holdings
data.description: In a Binance Square Post, Yi He, co-founder of Binance opened her personal trading profile to the public, offering a rare glimpse into her investment portfolio. Her post also revealed that her holdings outside of BNB are largely derived from airdrops accumulated over time, showcasing her confidence in Binance's ecosystem and its long-term growth potential.

data.title: TikTok resumes online in U.S. Apple and Google app stores
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News: Short video social media platform TikTok confirmed on the 14th that the TikTok application has been restored to the US Apple and Google app stores since the evening of the 13th. Reporters saw in both app stores that the TikTok application has resumed normal use. (Xinhua News Agency)

data.title: TSMC May Acquire Intel's Chip Manufacturing Operations
data.description: According to Odaily, reports from U.S. media suggest that chip manufacturing giant TSMC is considering taking over Intel's chip manufacturing business. Sources indicate that Intel's board began discussions with TSMC late last year to explore potential collaboration in response to deteriorating business conditions. The negotiations are in the early stages, and the form of cooperation has not been determined. TSMC might hold a majority stake in Intel's chip manufacturing operations alongside other investors. It remains unclear how much of Intel's manufacturing business TSMC will acquire or the amount of investment involved. Sources suggest that the deal could be limited to Intel's production facilities in Oregon, Arizona, and New Mexico in the United States, as well as in countries like Ireland and Israel.

data.title: Goldman Sachs Reduces Grayscale Bitcoin Trust Holdings
data.description: According to Odaily, regulatory filings submitted to the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission reveal that Goldman Sachs has reduced its holdings in the Grayscale Bitcoin Trust (GBTC) to approximately $3.6 million. This adjustment comes amid increased investments in Bitcoin ETFs by BlackRock and Fidelity. Grayscale's official data indicates that as of February 14, GBTC's Bitcoin holdings have decreased to around 200,118.5305 BTC, with a market value of $19.45 billion.

data.title: Hong Kong Securities Regulator Considers Leveraged Cryptocurrency ETFs
data.description:
According to PANews, the Hong Kong Securities and Futures Commission (SFC) has previously approved the listing of Bitcoin and Ethereum futures and spot exchange-traded funds (ETFs). In response to inquiries about the potential approval of leveraged and inverse (L&I) cryptocurrency ETFs, SFC Chief Executive Officer Julia Leung stated that the commission would consider the volatility of the assets. If the volatility is not significant, the SFC may approve the issuance of such leveraged and inverse products.

Additionally, Leung mentioned that the SFC is preparing to issue a circular allowing issuers to launch ETFs based on alternative investments. This circular will include measures to protect investors.

data.title: LBank has launched STRDY U-margined perpetual contract
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News According to the official announcement, LBank has launched the STRDY U-based perpetual contract at 15:00 (SGT) on February 15, 2025, with a maximum leverage of 50 times.
